Title: Mitochondrial formate dehydrogenase links mitochondrial and cytosolic one carbon metabolism via a one-carbon shunt

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAD-dependent formate dehydrogenase; mitochondrial targeting peptide at N-terminus
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: One-carbon metabolism; folate-dependent C1 metabolism; mitochondrial formate oxidation/C1 shunt
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mitochondrial formate oxidation branch linking mitochondrial and cytosolic one-carbon metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Side-route/regulatory shunt in mitochondrial one-carbon metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Controls flux of formate exchange between mitochondria and cytosol; competes with/redirects formate between oxidation to CO2 and assimilation into folate-dependent C1 metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes formate to CO2 using NAD+ as electron acceptor, producing NADH; shows minor activity with glyoxylate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains homeostatic one-carbon metabolism by controlling flux across a mitochondrial–cytosolic C1 shunt; FDH1 loss impairs growth and photosynthesis and causes hypersensitivity to exogenous formate, whereas overexpression promotes growth and increases formate resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports biosynthesis of nucleotides, amino acids, cofactors, methylation reactions, and overall cellular one-carbon homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mitochondria and cytosol via proposed formate shunt
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primarily expressed in cotyledons, apex of true leaf vascular bundles, and root differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in cotyledons, apex of true leaf vascular bundles, and root differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient CO2; low CO2 photorespiratory conditions; short-day conditions; exogenous sodium formate treatment (5 mM)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 constitutive promoter for overexpression; native FDH1 promoter for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and enzyme assay; GFP/mCherry localization; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout mutants; overexpression lines; mitochondrial activity assays; in vitro mutant protein assay; metabolite profiling; gas exchange; chlorophyll fluorescence
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Mitochondrial proteomics dataset cited for transit peptide cleavage (Kuhnert et al. 2020); steady-state metabolomics by GC-EI-QTOF-MS; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point/regulatory shunt controlling C1 flux, but not established as a classical committed or rate-limiting biosynthetic enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: FDH1 overexpression improved growth, photosynthetic performance, and tolerance to exogenous formate, suggesting value for engineering more robust one-carbon metabolism and growth; deployment should consider altered root length and broader metabolic network context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 1. Recombinant FDH1 is NAD-dependent with Km for formate about 3.85 mM and no activity with NADP. 2. No dual chloroplast localization was supported. 3. No allosteric regulation by tested photorespiratory metabolites or redox conditions was observed. 4. Steady-state pools of glycine, serine, methionine, folate, formate, and SAM were unchanged, implying FDH1 mainly affects flux rather than pool size. 5. FDH1 may be post-translationally regulated; lysine acetylation was reported previously but K184R mimic showed no activity change in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: FDH1 overexpression reduced primary root length; knockout reduced growth, photosynthesis, germination in some alleles, and chlorophyll a/b ratio
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FDH1 encodes a mitochondrial NAD-dependent formate dehydrogenase that oxidizes formate to CO2 and produces NADH. 2. CRISPR loss-of-function fdh1 mutants lacked mitochondrial FDH activity, were hypersensitive to exogenous formate, and showed reduced growth and photosynthetic capacity. 3. FDH1 overexpression conferred formate resistance, accelerated growth, and increased photosynthetic parameters. 4. Despite strong physiological phenotypes, steady-state levels of major C1-related metabolites were unchanged, supporting a flux-control role. 5. The study proposes FDH1 regulates a mitochondrial–cytosolic one-carbon shunt via formate exchange.
